text: Sleep deprivation negatively affects reaction time, accuracy, vigor, submaximal strength, and endurance in athletes.
Sleep deprivation impairs cognitive functions including judgment and decision-making, which can affect athletic performance.
Sleep extension can improve reaction times, mood, sprint times, tennis serve accuracy, swim turns, kick stroke efficiency, and basketball shooting accuracy.
Banking sleep (extending sleep prior to a night of intentional sleep deprivation before a sporting event) may improve subsequent performance.
Improving sleep quality through sleep hygiene practices is a simple and effective strategy for enhancing both athlete health and athletic performance, preferable to supplement use.
Athletes seeking to combat sleep deprivation may turn to supplements that carry potentially serious side effects, making sleep hygiene the safer intervention.
